posts.title: 5 Seconds Is All You Get
posts.imagePrompt: person scrolling phone looking frustrated, close up hands on smartphone, warm indoor lighting

facebook.copy:
A visitor lands on your website. You have about 5 seconds before they decide to stay or leave.

Here is what makes them leave:

- The page takes forever to load
- They can not tell what you do within 3 seconds
- It looks broken on their phone
- There is no way to call, book, or message you

Here is the thing. Most of these are fixable in an afternoon.

The businesses that show up first on Google are not always the best at what they do. They just made it easy for customers to find them and take the next step.

If your website has not been updated in a while, it might be working against you without you even knowing it.

Eric Lefler
OphidianAI
eric.lefler@ophidianai.com
facebook.altText: Person scrolling on phone with frustrated expression

instagram.copy:
A visitor lands on your website. You have about 5 seconds before they decide to stay or leave.

Swipe to see the 4 things that make them leave -- and what to do about it.

Save this post if your website has not been updated in a while. These fixes can make a real difference.

Eric Lefler -- OphidianAI -- eric.lefler@ophidianai.com

#SmallBusiness #WebDesign #ColumbusIndiana #SouthernIndiana #SmallBizTips #WebsiteTips #LocalBusiness
instagram.altText: Carousel: 5 seconds is all you get - what makes visitors leave your website

slides.text:
5 Seconds Is All You Get

What makes a visitor stay or leave your website

Slow load time

If your site takes more than 3 seconds to load, over half your visitors are already gone

Unclear message

Visitors need to know what you do and where you are within 3 seconds of landing

Broken on phones

More than half of all website visits come from phones. If yours does not work on mobile, you are invisible to most people

No next step

If there is no way to call, book, or message you, visitors leave and find someone who makes it easy

Save this for later

posts.title: Indiana Businesses Without Websites
posts.imagePrompt: small town main street Indiana storefronts, American small business downtown, warm afternoon light

facebook.copy:
I have been researching small businesses across Indiana for the past few weeks.

What I have found is surprising. There are businesses with hundreds of five-star reviews, loyal customers for 20+ years, and real expertise -- but when you search for them online, they do not show up.

Some have no website at all. Others have a site that has not been touched since it was built years ago. A few have placeholder text still showing on the homepage.

These are good businesses. They are just invisible to anyone who searches online before making a decision -- which is most people now.

If you own a business in Indiana and are not sure how your online presence looks to a stranger, drop your website in the comments. I will take a quick look and let you know what I see. No pitch, just honest feedback.

Eric Lefler
OphidianAI
eric.lefler@ophidianai.com
facebook.altText: Small town main street with local business storefronts

instagram.copy:
I have been researching small businesses across Indiana for the past few weeks.

Businesses with hundreds of five-star reviews, decades of loyal customers, real expertise -- completely invisible online.

No website. Or a website that has not been updated in years. Placeholder text still showing on the homepage.

These are great businesses. They are just impossible to find if you search online first -- which most people do now.

If you own a business in Indiana, drop your URL in the comments. I will take a quick look and share what I see. No pitch. Just honest feedback.

Eric Lefler
OphidianAI
eric.lefler@ophidianai.com
